content.text:
Please analyze this codebase and create a CLAUDE.md file, which will be given to future instances of Claude Code to operate in this repository.

What to add:
1. Commands that will be commonly used, such as how to build, lint, and run tests. Include the necessary commands to develop in this codebase, such as how to run a single test.
2. High-level code architecture and structure so that future instances can be productive more quickly. Focus on the "big picture" architecture that requires reading multiple files to understand.

Usage notes:
- If there's already a CLAUDE.md, suggest improvements to it.
- When you make the initial CLAUDE.md, do not repeat yourself and do not include obvious instructions like "Provide helpful error messages to users", "Write unit tests for all new utilities", "Never include sensitive information (API keys, tokens) in code or commits".
- Avoid listing every component or file structure that can be easily discovered.
- Don't include generic development practices.
- If there are Cursor rules (in .cursor/rules/ or .cursorrules) or Copilot rules (in .github/copilot-instructions.md), make sure to include the important parts.
- If there is a README.md, make sure to include the important parts.
- Do not make up information such as "Common Development Tasks", "Tips for Development", "Support and Documentation" unless this is expressly included in other files that you read.
- Be sure to prefix the file with the following text:

```
# CLAUDE.md

This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.
```
I'll analyze the codebase to understand its architecture and create a comprehensive CLAUDE.md file. Let me start by exploring the project structure and key files.

{
  "name": "zephyr-web",
  "version": "0.1.0",
  "private": true,
  "license": "Apache-2.0",
  "scripts": {
    "dev": "vite",
    "build": "vite build",
    "preview": "vite preview",
    "lint": "eslint 'src/**/*.{js,jsx,ts,tsx}'",
    "lint:fix": "eslint 'src/**/*.{js,jsx,ts,tsx}' --fix",
    "test": "vitest run",
    "test:watch": "vitest",
    "test:e2e": "playwright test",
    "postinstall": "playwright install"
  },
  "dependencies": {
    "@dnd-kit/core": "6.3.1",
    "@dnd-kit/sortable": "10.0.0",
    "@preact/signals-react": "3.6.0",
    "@sentry/cli": "2.58.2",
    "@sentry/react": "10.25.0",
    "@sentry/tracing": "7.120.4",
    "@tanstack/react-query": "5.90.10",
    "aws-amplify": "6.15.8",
    "axios": "1.13.2",
    "classnames": "2.5.1",
    "path-to-regexp": "8.3.0",
    "react": "19.2.0",
    "react-datepicker": "8.9.0",
    "react-dom": "19.2.0",
    "react-hook-form": "7.66.1",
    "react-icons": "5.5.0",
    "react-plaid-link": "4.1.1",
    "react-router-dom": "7.9.6",
    "universal-cookie": "8.0.1",
    "web-vitals": "4.2.4"
  },
  "devDependencies": {
    "@playwright/test": "1.56.1",
    "@testing-library/dom": "10.4.1",
    "@testing-library/jest-dom": "6.9.1",
    "@testing-library/react": "16.3.0",
    "@testing-library/user-event": "14.6.1",
    "@types/jest": "30.0.0",
    "@types/node": "22.14.0",
    "@types/react": "19.2.6",
    "@types/react-dom": "19.2.3",
    "@vitejs/plugin-react-swc": "^4.2.2",
    "@vitest/coverage-v8": "^4.0.13",
    "dotenv": "17.2.3",
    "eslint-plugin-react": "7.37.5",
    "eslint-plugin-react-hooks": "7.0.1",
    "globals": "16.5.0",
    "jsdom": "^27.2.0",
    "sass": "1.86.3",
    "typescript": "5.8.3",
    "vite": "^7.2.4",
    "vitest": "^4.0.13"
  },
  "browserslist": {
    "production": [
      ">0.2%",
      "not dead",
      "not op_mini all"
    ],
    "development": [
      "last 1 chrome version",
      "last 1 firefox version",
      "last 1 safari version"
    ]
  }
}

file.content:
{
  "compilerOptions": {
    "baseUrl": "./src",
    "target": "es5",

    "lib": ["dom", "dom.iterable", "esnext"],

    "allowJs": true,
    "skipLibCheck": true,
    "esModuleInterop": true,
    "allowSyntheticDefaultImports": true,
    "strict": true,
    "forceConsistentCasingInFileNames": true,
    "noFallthroughCasesInSwitch": true,
    "module": "ESNext",
    "moduleResolution": "bundler",
    "resolveJsonModule": true,
    "isolatedModules": true,
    "noEmit": true,
    "jsx": "react-jsx",

    "paths": {
      "Assets/*": ["assets/*"],
      "Repositories/*": ["repositories/*"],
      "Services/*": ["services/*"],
      "Components/*": ["components/*"],
      "Shared/*": ["shared/*"]
    },
    "types": ["vitest/globals", "node"],
    "sourceMap": true,
    "inlineSources": true,

    // Set `sourceRoot` to "/" to strip the build path prefix
    // from generated source code references.
    // This improves issue grouping in Sentry.
    "sourceRoot": "/"
  },
  "include": ["src"]
}

input.content:
# CLAUDE.md

This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.

## Project Overview

This is the React frontend client for Zephyr Budget App, a budget tracking application. The client is built with React 19, TypeScript, Vite, and uses Vitest for testing.

## Development Commands

### Running the Application
- `yarn dev` - Start development server with hot reload
- `yarn build` - Build production bundle
- `yarn preview` - Preview production build

### Testing
- `yarn test` - Run all unit tests (Vitest)
- `yarn test:watch` - Run tests in watch mode
- `yarn test:e2e` - Run end-to-end tests (Playwright)

### Linting
- `yarn lint` - Check for linting errors
- `yarn lint:fix` - Auto-fix linting errors

## Architecture

### Directory Structure

```
src/
├── app/                    # Application setup and configuration
│   ├── routes/            # Route definitions and guards (OnlyPrivate/OnlyPublic)
│   ├── errorBoundary/     # Error handling components
│   ├── notification/      # Notification system
│   ├── sentry/           # Sentry error tracking setup
│   └── styles/           # Global styles
├── pages/                 # Page-level components (e.g., TransactionsPage, BudgetPage)
├── components/            # Reusable UI components
│   ├── form/             # Form components (AuButton, AuField, AuDropdown, etc.)
│   ├── layouts/          # Layout components (BaseLayout, MainLayout)
│   ├── modal/            # Modal system
│   ├── sidebar/          # Sidebar system
│   └── svg/              # SVG icon components
├── repositories/          # Data fetching hooks using React Query
│   ├── transactions/
│   ├── budgets/
│   ├── bankAccounts/
│   └── category/
├── services/              # Business logic and utilities
│   ├── aiAssistant/
│   ├── authService.ts
│   ├── sidebarService.ts
│   ├── modalService.ts
│   └── notificationService.ts
└── shared/                # Shared utilities and types
    ├── types/
    ├── store/            # Global state management using @preact/signals
    ├── requestClient/    # Axios HTTP client
    └── utils/
```

### Key Architectural Patterns

#### Path Aliases
The project uses TypeScript path aliases (configured in tsconfig.json and vite.config.ts):
- `Assets/*` → `src/assets/*`
- `Repositories/*` → `src/repositories/*`
- `Services/*` → `src/services/*`
- `Components/*` → `src/components/*`
- `Shared/*` → `src/shared/*`

Always use these absolute imports instead of relative paths.

#### State Management
Global state is managed using `@preact/signals-react` through `globalStore`:
- State is defined in `src/shared/store/globalStore.ts`
- Access state with `useGlobalValue(GlobalStoreFieldE.FIELD_NAME)`
- Update state using service functions (e.g., `sidebarService`, `modalService`, `notificationService`)
- Services use `createGlobalValueSetter` to create updater functions

#### Data Fetching
Data fetching uses `@tanstack/react-query` with repository hooks:
- Repository hooks are in `src/repositories/*/` directories
- Named pattern: `useLoadX`, `useCreateX`, `useEditX`, `useDeleteX`
- All API calls go through `requestClient` in `src/shared/requestClient/`
- API endpoints are defined in `ApiGatewayE` enum

#### Routing
- Routes defined in `src/app/routes/AppRoutes.tsx`
- Uses React Router v7 with Sentry integration
- Route guards: `OnlyPrivate` (authenticated) and `OnlyPublic` (unauthenticated)
- Path constants defined in `src/shared/constants/appPaths`

#### UI Patterns

**Sidebars:**
- Managed by `sidebarService` in `src/services/sidebarService.ts`
- Service provides: `open()`, `openFirst()`, `close()`, `closeAll()`, `finalizeClose()`
- Sidebars support stacking and graceful closing animations
- Sidebar types defined in `SidebarNameE` and props in `SidebarPropsT`

**Modals:**
- Managed by `modalService` (similar pattern to sidebars)

**Notifications:**
- Managed by `notificationService`
- Toast-style notifications using global signal state

#### Component Naming
- Pages: `*Page.tsx` (e.g., `TransactionsPage.tsx`)
- Form components prefixed with `Au` (e.g., `AuButton`, `AuField`, `AuDropdown`)
- Layout components: `*Layout.tsx`
- Sidebar components: `*Sidebar.tsx`
- SVG components: `*Svg.tsx`

#### TypeScript Conventions
- Components use arrow function syntax: `const MyComponent = () => {...}`
- Prefer object destructuring for props and variables
- Prefer private constructor fields
- Types suffixed with `T` (e.g., `TransactionFiltersT`)
- DTOs suffixed with `DTO` (e.g., `TransactionDTO`)
- Enums suffixed with `E` (e.g., `GlobalStoreFieldE`)
- Send DTOs (for API requests) suffixed with `SDTO` (e.g., `CreateTransactionSDTO`)

## Testing Strategy

- Tests placed in `__tests__` directories next to code being tested
- Unit test setup in `src/setupTests.ts`
- E2E tests in `tests/` directory (excluded from Vitest)
- Coverage with Vitest + v8 provider
- Use coverage ignore comments for simple functions: `/* istanbul ignore next -- @preserve */`

## Environment Configuration

- Environment variables loaded from parent directory (`envDir: '..'` in vite.config.ts)
- Backend API proxied through Vite dev server at `/api`
- Frontend port: `VITE_APP_WEB_PORT`
- Backend URL: `VITE_APP_API_BASE_URL` or `SERVER_URL:SERVER_PORT`

## Important Notes

- Never create files unnecessarily; prefer editing existing files
- Never proactively create documentation or README files
- Component must use arrow functions (enforced by ESLint)
- React import not needed in JSX files (React 19)
- Tests must pass before considering work complete
- Use Sentry for error tracking (configured in `src/app/sentry/`)

content.text:
I've created a comprehensive CLAUDE.md file for the client package at `/Users/dmitry/WebstormProjects/zephyr-budget-app/client/CLAUDE.md`.

The file includes:

1. **Development Commands** - All essential yarn commands for dev, build, test, and lint
2. **Architecture Overview** - Complete directory structure with explanations
3. **Key Architectural Patterns:**
   - Path aliases configuration
   - State management using @preact/signals
   - Data fetching with React Query and repository hooks
   - Routing setup with guards
   - UI patterns for sidebars, modals, and notifications
   - Component naming conventions
   - TypeScript conventions (suffixes for types, DTOs, enums)

4. **Testing Strategy** - Where tests go and how to use coverage
5. **Environment Configuration** - How the dev server and API proxy work
6. **Important Notes** - Key reminders about file creation, testing, and coding standards

The documentation focuses on the high-level architecture and patterns that require reading multiple files to understand, avoiding obvious details that can be discovered through code exploration.
